entries.authors: Anel, M.; Biedermann, G.; Finster, E.; Joyal, A.
entries.title: Goodwillie's calculus of functors and higher topos theory
entries.note: P_n as a left exact modality on functor ∞-topoi; Blakers–Massey for the Goodwillie tower; the precise boundary of the sheafification analogy (non-topological localizations).

entries.title: A generalization of Snaith-type filtration
entries.note: Explicit model for the Taylor tower of Σ^∞Map(K, X); generalizes stable splittings of mapping spaces

entries.title: The Weiss derivatives of BO(−) and BU(−)
entries.note: The fundamental worked example of orthogonal calculus: all derivative spectra of BO and BU and the homology of the layers.

entries.title: Operads and chain rules for the calculus of functors
entries.note: The chain rule: derivatives of a composite via composition product; ∂∗Id as an operad — the homotopical Faà di Bruno formula.

entries.title: Goodwillie calculus
entries.venue: Handbook of Homotopy Theory (H. Miller, ed.), Chapman & Hall/CRC, 2020, 1–38
entries.note: The standard modern survey; precise ∞-categorical statements of all foundational theorems with attributions

entries.authors: Arone, G. Z.; Dwyer, W. G.
entries.title: Partition complexes, Tits buildings and symmetric products
entries.venue: Proceedings of the London Mathematical Society 82 (2001), 229–256
entries.note: Identifies the homology of layers via Steinberg idempotents and symmetric powers of spheres — the L(k) description

entries.title: The Goodwillie tower of the identity functor and the unstable periodic homotopy of spheres
entries.note: Prime-power layers of the identity tower at spheres; v_k-periodic finiteness — flagship application

entries.title: Factorization homology of topological manifolds
entries.note: ⊗-excision / Eilenberg–Steenrod characterization of factorization homology — the covariant (cosheaf) mirror of manifold calculus.

entries.title: A factorization homology primer
entries.venue: Handbook of Homotopy Theory (H. Miller, ed.), CRC Press, 2020
entries.note: Explicit Weiss-cover and Weiss-cosheaf language: factorization algebras as cosheaves for the Weiss topology.

entries.title: Comparing the orthogonal and homotopy functor calculi
entries.note: Precise comparison of the two calculi; the towers agree on analytic functors — good for the unifying-frameworks discussion.

entries.title: Model categories for orthogonal calculus
entries.note: Model-categorical foundations; upgrades Weiss's classification to a zig-zag of Quillen equivalences with O(n)-spectra.

entries.authors: Bauer, K.; Burke, M.; Ching, M.
entries.title: Tangent ∞-categories and Goodwillie calculus
entries.venue: arXiv preprint (v1 January 2021; v5 June 2026); no verified journal publication as of July 2026
entries.note: The unifying framework: Goodwillie tangent structure on differentiable ∞-categories, extending Rosický/Cockett–Cruttwell tangent categories; n-excisive functors as n-jets.

entries.authors: Bauer, K.; Johnson, B.; McCarthy, R. (appendix by Eldred, R.)
entries.title: Cross effects and calculus in an unbased setting
entries.note: Extends cross effects and the discrete tower to unbased/relative settings (functors on C_f) and compares with the Goodwillie tower.

entries.title: The Goodwillie tower for S¹ and Kuhn's theorem
entries.note: Calculus form of the 2-primary Whitehead conjecture: Whitehead sequence contracts the S¹-tower

entries.title: The Goodwillie tower and the EHP sequence
entries.venue: Memoirs of the American Mathematical Society 218 (2012), no. 1026
entries.note: Interlocks Goodwillie and EHP spectral sequences at p = 2; recomputes stems through the Toda range

entries.authors: Biedermann, G.; Chorny, B.; Röndigs, O.
entries.title: Calculus of functors and model categories
entries.note: P_n as left Bousfield localization; n-homogeneous model structure Quillen equivalent to Σₙ-spectra on finitary functors.

entries.authors: Bauer, K.; Johnson, B.; Osborne, C.; Riehl, E.; Tebbe, A.
entries.title: Directional derivatives and higher order chain rules for abelian functor calculus
entries.note: Abelian functor calculus is a cartesian differential category; Faà di Bruno-style higher chain rule — the bridge to differential categories.

entries.authors: Boavida de Brito, P.; Horel, G.
entries.title: Galois symmetries of knot spaces
entries.note: p-local vanishing of GW spectral-sequence differentials; T_{n+1} is a p-local universal Vassiliev invariant of degree ≤ n for n ≤ p+1; formality/Galois techniques.

entries.authors: Boavida de Brito, P.; Weiss, M.
entries.title: Manifold calculus and homotopy sheaves
entries.note: THE reference for polynomial approximation = homotopy J_k-sheafification (Weiss topologies); enriched manifold calculus; tower limit as operad module maps.

entries.authors: Boavida de Brito, P.; Weiss, M.
entries.title: Spaces of smooth embeddings and configuration categories
entries.note: The tower as derived maps of configuration categories; operadic description of immersion-to-embedding obstructions; basis for the Galois-symmetry results.

entries.title: Little cubes and long knots
entries.note: K ≃ C₂(P ⊔ {∗}): the space of long knots is the free little-2-cubes algebra on prime knots; JSJ ↔ cubes action; space-level Schubert theorem.

entries.authors: Budney, R.; Conant, J.; Koytcheff, R.; Sinha, D.
entries.title: Embedding calculus knot invariants are of finite type
entries.note: ev_n is an additive type-(n−1) invariant; E² in total degree 0 = primitive chord diagrams over ℤ; states the integral universality conjecture.

entries.authors: Budney, R.; Conant, J.; Scannell, K. P.; Sinha, D.
entries.title: New perspectives on self-linking
entries.note: First three mapping space models for classical knots; the third yields c₂ (type-2, second Conway coefficient) via quadrisecant/collinearity counts. NB: title is "self-linking", not "self-intersection".

entries.title: Bar constructions for topological operads and the Goodwillie derivatives of the identity
entries.note: Operad structure on ∂∗Id inducing the Lie operad in homology — the structured refinement of the derivatives

entries.title: A chain rule for Goodwillie derivatives of functors from spectra to spectra
entries.note: Full chain rule in the stable setting: derivatives of a composite are the composition product of derivatives.

entries.authors: Dundas, B. I.; Goodwillie, T. G.; McCarthy, R.
entries.title: The Local Structure of Algebraic K-Theory
entries.note: Definitive integral form of the DGM theorem: K/TC square homotopy cartesian for π₀-surjections with nilpotent kernel

entries.title: Homologie nicht-additiver Funktoren. Anwendungen
entries.note: Derived functors of non-additive functors (Symⁿ, Λⁿ, …); their stable derived functors are the algebraic model of linearization

entries.title: Relative K-theory and topological cyclic homology
entries.note: Extends McCarthy's theorem toward the integral statement for (simplicial) rings and FSPs

entries.title: Stable K-theory and topological Hochschild homology
entries.note: K^S ≃ THH: THH is the derivative of K-theory — the engine of trace methods

entries.title: On the groups H(Π, n). II: Methods of computation
entries.note: Introduces cross-effects and polynomial degree of functors — the algebraic prehistory of excision

entries.title: Relative algebraic K-theory and cyclic homology
entries.note: Rational relative K ≅ shifted relative cyclic homology for nilpotent extensions — rational half of DGM

entries.title: Calculus I: The first derivative of pseudoisotopy theory
entries.note: Origin of the derivative of a homotopy functor at a space; computes the derivative of pseudoisotopy/A-theory (∂_Y A ≃ Σ^∞(ΩY)₊)

entries.title: The differential calculus of homotopy functors
entries.venue: Proceedings of the ICM (Kyoto, 1990), Vol. I, 621–630, Math. Soc. Japan, Tokyo
entries.note: Ten-page announcement of the whole program — ideal first reading for students

entries.title: Calculus II: Analytic functors
entries.note: Stable n-excision Eₙ(c,κ), ρ-analyticity, higher Blakers–Massey (Thm 2.3); Id and A are 1-analytic

entries.title: Calculus III: Taylor series
entries.note: The Tₙ/Pₙ constructions, Taylor tower, classification of homogeneous functors by spectra with Σₙ-action, convergence Thm 1.13; circulated ~a decade before publication

entries.authors: Goodwillie, T. G.; Klein, J. R.
entries.title: Multiple disjunction for spaces of smooth embeddings
entries.note: The analyticity input behind the (k(n−m−2)+1−m)-connectivity estimate — the 'radius of convergence' of embedding calculus.

entries.title: Embeddings from the point of view of immersion theory: Part II
entries.note: Convergence of the embedding tower in codimension ≥ 3, granted the disjunction results of Goodwillie–Klein.

entries.title: Lie algebras and vₙ-periodic spaces
entries.note: vₙ-periodic unstable homotopy ≃ spectral Lie algebras in T(n)-local spectra — the modern culmination of the ∂_*(Id) story

entries.title: Goodwillie approximations to higher categories
entries.venue: Memoirs of the American Mathematical Society 272 (2021), no. 1333
entries.note: Categorified Taylor tower: a tower of ∞-categories from spaces to spectra; classification by derivatives of the identity.

entries.title: The derivatives of homotopy theory
entries.note: First identification of the derivatives of the identity functor via partition-complex models

entries.title: Linearization, Dold–Puppe stabilization, and Mac Lane's Q-construction
entries.note: Shows the three classical linearization procedures agree — the degree-1 bridge between Goodwillie calculus and homological algebra.

entries.title: Deriving calculus with cotriples
entries.venue: Transactions of the American Mathematical Society 356 (2004), 757–803
entries.note: Cotriple Taylor tower for functors to abelian categories, bridging Dold–Puppe with Goodwillie calculus — ideal for the warm-up section

entries.title: A chain rule in the calculus of homotopy functors
entries.note: First-derivative chain rule for homotopy functors of spaces; the result Arone–Ching later extended to all derivatives.

entries.title: Embedding calculus and grope cobordism of knots
entries.venue: Advances in Mathematics 451 (2024), Paper No. 109779, 118 pp.
entries.note: Surjectivity of ev_n for long knots in any 3-manifold via grope cobordism; with Boavida–Horel gives rational universality — the surjectivity half of the ℤ conjecture.

entries.title: Diffeomorphisms of discs and the second Weiss derivative of BTop(−)
entries.venue: arXiv preprint (v2 June 2026); reported to appear in Invent. Math. — verify final details before citing print data
entries.note: State-of-the-art application: second rational Weiss derivative of BTop(−) computes rational homotopy of diffeomorphism groups of discs.

entries.title: Tate cohomology and periodic localization of polynomial functors
entries.note: T(n)-local Tate vanishing ⇒ all Taylor towers of functors Sp → Sp split telescopically (the Kuhn splitting)

entries.title: Localization of André–Quillen–Goodwillie towers, and the periodic homology of infinite loopspaces
entries.note: K(n)-homology of Ω^∞X from the split tower — the main computational payoff of the splitting

entries.title: Goodwillie towers and chromatic homotopy: an overview
entries.venue: Proc. Nishida Fest (Kinosaki 2003), Geom. Topol. Monogr. 10 (2007), 245–279
entries.note: Survey linking Taylor towers to periodic/chromatic homotopy; readable account of the Σ^∞Ω^∞ tower and Snaith splitting

entries.authors: Lambrechts, P.; Turchin, V.; Volić, I.
entries.title: The rational homology of spaces of long knots in codimension > 2
entries.note: For d ≥ 4 the rational Vassiliev/Sinha homology spectral sequence collapses at E¹ via relative formality of little d-disks; H⁎(Emb̄;ℚ) = Hochschild homology of the Poisson operad.

entries.title: Higher Algebra (Chapter 6: The Calculus of Functors)
entries.venue: unpublished book, available from the author's webpage
entries.note: ∞-categorical foundations: Pₙ as left adjoint/localization (Thm 6.1.1.10); spectra as excisive functors (§1.4.2)

entries.title: Non collapse of the Sinha spectral sequence for knots in ℝ³
entries.venue: arXiv preprint (2025) — preprint, not yet peer-reviewed
entries.note: Computer-assisted nonzero d₃ in the mod-2 Sinha spectral sequence for d = 3 — sharp contrast with rational collapse (LTV); flag preprint status if cited.

entries.title: Relative algebraic K-theory and topological cyclic homology
entries.note: Relative K ≃ relative TC (profinitely) for nilpotent extensions of simplicial rings, proved with Goodwillie calculus

entries.title: Introduction to the manifold calculus of Goodwillie–Weiss
entries.note: Best short survey for students: analogies with ordinary calculus, polynomial functors, layers, convergence — ideal companion reading for a course tab.

entries.title: Cubical Homotopy Theory
entries.venue: New Mathematical Monographs 25, Cambridge University Press
entries.note: Textbook treatment of cubes, homotopy (co)limits, Blakers–Massey, and an introduction to both functor calculi — best classroom prerequisite source

entries.title: A streamlined proof of Goodwillie's n-excisive approximation
entries.note: Two-page simplification of Calc III Lemma 1.9, the key step in Pₙ being n-excisive — good for a proof-sketch collapsible

entries.title: Operads and knot spaces
entries.venue: Journal of the American Mathematical Society 19 (2006), no. 2, 461–486
entries.note: Kontsevich-operad cosimplicial model: Totⁿ ≃ T_nEmb̄; little-2-cubes action on long knots modulo immersions via McClure–Smith.

entries.title: The topology of spaces of knots: cosimplicial models
entries.note: Punctured-knots and mapping-space models for the tower stages, weakly equivalent to knot spaces in ambient dimension ≥ 4; source of the Sinha spectral sequence.

entries.title: A stable decomposition of ΩⁿSⁿX
entries.venue: Journal of the London Mathematical Society (2) 7 (1974), 577–583
entries.note: The Snaith splitting — the split Taylor tower of Σ^∞Ω^∞ on suspension spectra

entries.title: Finite type knot invariants and the calculus of functors
entries.note: Over ℝ, type-n invariants factor through T_{2n}; the tower is a universal real finite-type invariant (via Bott–Taubes configuration space integrals).

entries.title: Algebraic K-theory of spaces, concordance, and stable homotopy theory
entries.venue: Algebraic Topology and Algebraic K-theory, Annals of Mathematics Studies 113, Princeton University Press (1987), 392–417
entries.note: Stable A-theory ≃ stable homotopy: the linearization of A(X) and the Σ^∞(X₊) × Wh^Diff splitting

entries.title: Orthogonal calculus
entries.note: Founding paper of orthogonal calculus: polynomial functors on inner-product spaces, O(n)-derivative spectra, classification of homogeneous functors; see also the 1998 erratum.

entries.title: Calculus of embeddings
entries.note: Gentle survey; the best source for the immersion-theory-as-linearization and jet analogies for teaching.

entries.title: Erratum: Orthogonal calculus
entries.note: Corrects an error in the 1995 classification argument — cite alongside the original when teaching the homogeneous classification.

entries.title: Embeddings from the point of view of immersion theory: Part I
entries.note: Foundations of manifold calculus: good functors, polynomial degree ≤ k, T_k via 𝒪_k, linear = sections of a fibration.

entries.title: Erratum to: Embeddings from the point of view of immersion theory: Part I
entries.note: Corrects the proof that T_kF is polynomial of degree ≤ k; always cite alongside Part I.

entries.title: Spectral algebra models of unstable vₙ-periodic homotopy theory
entries.venue: Bousfield Classes and Ohkawa's Theorem, Springer Proceedings in Mathematics & Statistics 309 (2020), 275–323
entries.note: Survey linking the identity tower, Bousfield–Kuhn functors and spectral Lie algebras — advanced outlook lecture

entries.authors: Arone, G.; Ching, M. (mentors); Talbot participants
entries.title: Talbot 2012: Calculus of Functors — workshop notes
entries.venue: MIT Talbot Workshop, notes available on the workshop page
entries.note: A complete week-long graduate syllabus (foundations → operads → all three calculi → applications) with free notes

entries.title: Goodwillie calculus — UT Austin graduate seminar notes, Fall 2017
entries.note: Student-run seminar notes; useful model for pacing a semester of talks

entries.title: Goodwillie calculus — graduate seminar program and notes, Spring 2012
entries.note: Compact seminar plan with talk-by-talk references, including analyticity and radii of convergence

entries.title: Arbeitsgemeinschaft mit aktuellem Thema: The Goodwillie Calculus of Functors
entries.venue: Oberwolfach Reports 17/2004, Mathematisches Forschungsinstitut Oberwolfach (meeting 0414, March–April 2004)
entries.note: Verified date correction: the Oberwolfach study group was 2004; its report is a ready-made talk sequence

entries.title: Goodwillie calculus seminar (close reading of Calculus III)
entries.venue: Utrecht University, fall 2022, schedule and talk summaries online
entries.note: Recent Utrecht course page; evidence for the 'read Calc III directly' teaching route

entries.title: Goodwillie Calculus — Topology Advanced Class, Hilary Term 2025
entries.note: Current course offering with abstract/schedule; confirms the subject is standard advanced-class material
